content:
100	
101	\begin{lemma}
102	[Universal property of an away-localisation]
103	  \label{lem:mathlib_away_lift}
104	  \lean{IsLocalization.Away.lift}
105	  \mathlibok
106	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
107	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(x \in R\), and \(S = R[1/x]\) a localisation
108	  of \(R\) away from \(x\). Given any ring homomorphism \(g : R \to P\) that
109	  sends \(x\) to a unit of \(P\), there is a unique ring homomorphism
110	  \(S \to P\) extending \(g\) along \(R \to S\).
111	\end{lemma}
112	
113	\subsection*{The transition map by matrix algebra}
114	
115	We now build \(\theta_{I,J}\) in seven steps over the localised chart ring
116	\(R^I_J := \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), splitting at the matrix-algebra seams. The
117	ambient chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) is the polynomial ring of
118	\cref{def:gr_affine_chart} in the \(d(r-d)\) free variables \(x^I_{p,q}\)
119	(\(p \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), \(q \notin I\)).
120	
121	\begin{definition}
122	
123	\leanok
124	[The universal matrix \(X^I\)]
125	  \label{def:gr_universal_matrix}
126	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix}
127	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart}
128	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
129	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
130	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L807-L821).
131	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
132	  % "For any subset $I\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(I) =d$,
133	  %  consider the $d\times r$ matrix $X^I$ whose $I$ the minor
134	  %  $X^I_I$ is the $d\times d$ identity matrix $1_{d\times d}$, while the remaining
135	  %  entries of $X^I$ are independent variables $x^I_{p,q}$ over $\Z$."
136	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
137	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). The \emph{universal
138	  matrix} \(X^I\) is the \(d \times r\) matrix over the chart ring
139	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) whose \(I\)-minor \(X^I_I\) is the \(d \times d\) identity
140	  \(1_{d\times d}\) and whose remaining entries are the free indeterminates
141	  \(x^I_{p,q}\) (\(1 \le p \le d\), \(q \notin I\)). It is the universal point of
142	  the chart \(U^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}): a ring map
143	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to A\) is the same as a \(d \times r\) matrix over \(A\)
144	  with identity \(I\)-minor.
145	\end{definition}
146	
147	\begin{definition}
148	
149	\leanok
150	[The minor determinant \(P^I_J\)]
151	  \label{def:gr_minor_det}
152	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.minorDet}
153	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix}
154	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
155	  % affine patches" (read from
156	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
157	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
158	  % "For any $J\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(J) =d$, let
159	  %  $P^I_J = \det(X^I_J) \in \Z[X^I]$
160	  %  where $X^I_J$ is the $J$ th minor of $X^I$."
161	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
162	  For \(J \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(J) = d\), let \(X^I_J\) denote the
163	  \(J\)-th minor of the universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix})
164	  -- the \(d \times d\) submatrix whose columns are those indexed by \(J\). Set
165	  \[
166	    P^I_J \;:=\; \det\bigl(X^I_J\bigr) \;\in\; \mathbb{Z}[X^I].
167	  \]
168	  Then \(R^I_J := \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) (the localisation of the chart ring
169	  away from \(P^I_J\)) is the ring of the principal open subscheme
170	  \(U^I_J = \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J] \subseteq U^I\) on which \(P^I_J\) is
171	  invertible. One has \(P^I_I = \det(1_{d\times d}) = 1\), so \(R^I_I =
172	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) and \(U^I_I = U^I\).
173	\end{definition}
174	
175	\begin{definition}
176	
177	\leanok
178	[The localised \(J\)-minor \(X^I_J\) over \(R^I_J\)]
179	  \label{def:gr_universal_minor}
180	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinor}
181	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_minor_det}
182	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
183	  % affine patches" (read from
184	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
185	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
186	  % "Let $U^I_J = \spec \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$
187	  %  the open subscheme of $U^I$ where $P^I_J$ is invertible."
188	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
189	  Write \(X^I_J \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(R^I_J)\) for the \(J\)-minor of
190	  the universal matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) with its entries
191	  pushed forward along the structure map \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to R^I_J\) into the
192	  localised chart ring \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\)
193	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}). By construction \(\det(X^I_J)\) is the image of
194	  \(P^I_J\) in \(R^I_J\).
195	\end{definition}
196	
197	\begin{lemma}
198	
199	\leanok
200	[The localised minor determinant is a unit]
201	  \label{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_det_universalMinor}
203	  \uses{def:gr_universal_minor, def:gr_minor_det, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
204	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
205	  % affine patches" (read from
206	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
207	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
208	  % "the open subscheme of $U^I$ where $P^I_J$ is invertible. This means
209	  %  the $d\times d$-matrix $X^I_J$ admits an inverse $(X^I_J)^{-1}$
210	  %  on $U^I_J$."
211	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
212	  The determinant of the localised \(J\)-minor \(X^I_J\)
213	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_minor}) is a unit of \(R^I_J\):
214	  \[
215	    \det\bigl(X^I_J\bigr) \in (R^I_J)^{\times}.
216	  \]
217	\end{lemma}
218	\begin{proof}
219	  \uses{def:gr_universal_minor, def:gr_minor_det, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
220	  \leanok
221	  By \cref{def:gr_universal_minor}, \(\det(X^I_J)\) is the image of \(P^I_J\)
222	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}) under the structure map
223	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to R^I_J\). Since \(R^I_J\) is the localisation away from
224	  \(P^I_J\), that image is a unit by
225	  \cref{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}.
226	\end{proof}
227	
228	\begin{definition}
229	
230	\leanok
231	[The Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\)]
232	  \label{def:gr_universalMinorInv}
233	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMinorInv}
234	  \uses{def:gr_universal_minor}
235	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
236	  % affine patches" (read from
237	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
238	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
239	  % "the $d\times d$-matrix $X^I_J$ admits an inverse $(X^I_J)^{-1}$
240	  %  on $U^I_J$."
241	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
242	  Let \((X^I_J)^{-1} \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(R^I_J)\) be the
243	  nonsingular (Cramer) inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor \(X^I_J\)
244	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_minor}). Its entries lie in \(R^I_J\) by Cramer's rule.
245	\end{definition}
246	
247	\begin{lemma}
248	
249	\leanok

711	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
712	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
713	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
714	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
715	\end{lemma}
716	
717	\begin{proof}
718	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
719	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
720	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
721	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
722	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
723	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
724	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
725	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
726	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
727	\end{proof}
728	
729	\begin{lemma}[Composite coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
730	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
731	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
732	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
733	  For composable scheme morphisms \(a : T'' \to T'\) and \(b : T' \to T\), the comparison
734	  morphism of unit modules \((b \circ a)^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) factors as
735	  \(a^{*}\) of the comparison for \(b\), the comparison for \(a\), and the component at
736	  \(\mathbf{1}\) of the iterated-pullback comparison \(a^{*}b^{*} \cong (b\circ a)^{*}\)
737	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}).
738	\end{lemma}
739	
740	\begin{proof}
741	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
742	  This is the composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}: the mate
743	  construction is compatible with composition of adjunctions, so the comparison for
744	  \(b \circ a\) is the pasting of the comparisons for \(a\) and \(b\) reassociated by the
745	  pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The pentagon coherence of the
746	  iterated-pullback comparisons makes the factorisation well-defined.
747	\end{proof}
748	
749	Together \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id} and \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
750	upgrade to the corresponding free-sheaf coherences -- the free-pullback isomorphism at the
751	identity equals the component of the pullback-identity isomorphism, and likewise for the
752	composite -- by coproduct extensionality over the decomposition
753	\(\mathcal{O}^{\,\oplus I} = \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\). These coherences discharge both
754	functoriality laws (identity and composition) of the Grassmannian
755	functor \cref{def:grassmannian_functor}.
756	
757	\begin{definition}
758	
759	\leanok
760	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
761	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
762	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
763	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
764	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
765	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
766	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
767	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
768	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
769	  % "represents the
770	  %  contravariant functor
771	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
772	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
773	  %  set of all equivalence classes
774	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
775	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$."
776	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
777	  The \emph{Grassmannian functor}
778	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d) = \Quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}
779	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/\mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) is the contravariant functor
780	  from schemes to sets defined as follows. For a scheme \(T\), a \emph{family of
781	  rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} is a pair
782	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a locally free
783	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) of rank \(d\) together with a surjective
784	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-linear homomorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
785	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Two such families
786	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
787	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
788	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
789	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
790	  \[
791	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
792	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
793	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
794	  \]
795	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
796	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
797	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
798	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
799	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
800	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
801	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
802	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
803	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
804	\end{definition}
805	
806	\section{The universal property}
807	\label{sec:grquot_universal}
808	
809	\begin{theorem}
810	
811	\leanok
812	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
813	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
814	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
815	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
816	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,

818	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
819	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
820	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
821	  % SOURCE QUOTE:

Its sibling `lem:mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_maximal` (the stalk-route *criterion* — also dead) is used only by `gf_stalk_flat_over_base` / `gf_stalk_flat_localBase`, which themselves are stranded dead-route nodes. The footnote comparison is the only surviving trace.
  - **Action for prover lane:** no action needed (none of the live B2 → G3 chain depends on it). Blueprint writer should remove in next pass.
- **6 lean_aux isolated nodes:** all proved (`✓`), 0 effort — library-level orphans, no action needed.

```
All three are in the QUOT lane, already tracked as open items (gap-related helpers). Not in current iter's critical path. No blueprint action needed now.

---

## 2. Focus chapters — HARD GATE verdicts

### 2.1 `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`

**Verdict: COMPLETE ✓ | CORRECT ✓ | HARD GATE: CLEARED**

#### B2 chain (new this iter)

| Label | Mathlib anchor | Stated |
|---|---|---|
| `lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq` (B2.1) | `Scheme.basicOpen_res` | D(g) = D(ḡ) under cross-chart match |
| `lem:gf_section_localization_twoleg` (B2.2) | — | Section-localization identity Γ(W,M)[1/g] ≅ Γ(W_j,M)[1/ḡ] |
| `lem:gf_base_localization_comparison` (B2.3) | — | Source-ring localization agrees with target-ring localization |
| `lem:gf_section_localization_flat_descent` (B2 assembly) | — | Flat descent along the two-leg comparison |
| `lem:gf_crossChart_spanning_cover` (B2.4) | — | Cross-chart basic opens span the cover |
| `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` (G3) | `mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span` | Final assembly: generic flatness |

**Q: Is the matched-pair (g, ḡ) treatment of B2.1/B2.2 mathematically sound?**

**YES.** The blueprint correctly identifies and addresses the subtlety: the bare image of `g ∈ Γ(X,W)` in `Bⱼ` does NOT automatically give `D(g) = D(ḡ)` over the overlap, because sections from different charts can agree on stalks without cutting out the same basic open over the full intersection. B2.1 (`gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq`) takes two explicit hypotheses:
1. `g|_O = ḡ|_O` (sections agree over the overlap O = W ∩ Wⱼ), AND
2. `D(g) ≤ O` and `D(ḡ) ≤ O` (both basic opens are contained in the overlap)

Under these hypotheses the equality `D(g) = D(ḡ)` (as subsets of X, equivalently as subschemes of O) follows from `Scheme.basicOpen_res` (the Mathlib anchor at `lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res`): restricting both sides to O and using the section equality yields the identity. The statement is tight — without the containment hypotheses the equality is false in general. Sound.

B2.2 (`gf_section_localization_twoleg`) then takes this as a datum (`hḡ : X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen ḡ`) and produces the iso `Γ(W,M)[1/g] ≅ Γ(Wⱼ,M)[1/ḡ]` via transport along the basic-open equality + quasi-coherence. Sound.

**Q: Does `gf_flat_locality_assembly` correctly consume the new chain?**

**YES.** The `\uses{}` list of `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` is:
```
gf_patch_free_imp_flat
gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase
gf_section_localization_flat_descent      ← B2 assembly
gf_crossChart_spanning_cover              ← B2.4
mathlib_flat_of_isLocalized_span          ← Mathlib span criterion
mathlib_flat_localization_preserves
mathlib_flat_of_free
gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections           ← GF-G1 (DONE)
qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen
```
The proof text feeds the span cover (B2.4) + section-localization flatness (B2 descent) into `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span`. The `gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase` provides the same-base localization identity needed to convert the cross-chart iso into the form the span criterion expects. No missing edge. Chain is correct.

**Mathlib anchor `lem:mathlib_scheme_basicOpen_res` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.basicOpen_res`:**
Verified real Mathlib declaration. States that for an open immersion `i : U → X` and `f : X.presheaf U`, `(i^* X).basicOpen (X.presheaf.map i.op f) = X.basicOpen f ∩ U`. Exactly what B2.1 needs.

**Dead stalk-route nodes still in chapter:** `gf_stalk_flat_over_base` (G3.2) and `gf_stalk_flat_localBase` (G3.4) are present but are not in the `\uses{}` chain of any live assembly lemma. They are benign (no broken edges, no impact on the live path). The isolated `lem:mathlib_flat_of_localized_maximal` anchor (see §1) is the only actionable cleanup item.

**No must-fix items for iter-055 prover.**

---

### 2.2 `Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex`

**Verdict: COMPLETE ✓ | CORRECT ✓ | HARD GATE: CLEARED**

Chapter has 21 nodes, 13 `\leanok`. The two new coherence lemmas are both unproved (expected — they are the prover's target this iter).

**Q: Is the `gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id` / `_comp` coherence block well-posed and formalizable?**

**YES.**

`lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id` (line 707–727):
- Statement: for `id_T`, the comparison morphism `id_T* 1 → 1` (the map underlying the free-pullback iso at a 1-element index) equals the `1`-component of the pullback-identity isomorphism `id_T* ≅ id`.
- Proof sketch: mate under pullback–pushforward adjunction; for identity the pushforward is the identity functor so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; the pullback-identity iso is the conjugate transpose of the pushforward-identity iso; tracing through gives equality.
- **Formalizability:** well-posed. Both the comparison morphism and the pullback-identity component live in the same hom-set; the proof is a mate calculation that Lean's `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.mate` API handles. Concrete Lean target: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`.

`lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (line 730–747):
- Statement: for composable `a : T'' → T'`, `b : T' → T`, the comparison `(b∘a)*1 → 1` factors as `a*(b's comparison) ≫ (a's comparison) ≫ (pullbackComp component at 1)`.
- Proof sketch: mate is compatible with composition of adjunctions; pentagon coherence of iterated-pullback comparisons (`def:modules_pullbackComp`, `\mathlibok` → `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`) makes the factorisation well-defined.
- **Formalizability:** well-posed. The `\uses{}` correctly lists both `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id` and `def:modules_pullbackComp`. The pentagon coherence is a Mathlib pseudofunctor law. Concrete Lean target: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`.

Together these two coherences unlock `def:grassmannian_functor` (already `\leanok` provisionally — its `\uses{}` lists both coherence lemmas) and transitively unblock the remaining functor-build work.

**No must-fix items.**

---

### 2.3 `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`

**Verdict: COMPLETE ✓ | CORRECT ✓ | HARD GATE: CLEARED**

Chapter has ~20 `\leanok` markers.

**Q: Is the promotion proof detailed enough to formalize?**

**YES.** The rewritten `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer` proof is structured as 3 explicit steps:

1. **Objectwise step:** `lem:relativeTensor_objectwise_coequalizer` (already `\leanok`) gives `isColimitCofork` for each `U`. All constituent declarations (`isColimitCofork`, `actN`, `actM`, `actLmap`, `actRmap`, `projL`, `piMor`, `coeq_condition`, `cofork`, `descHom`, `descMor`, `descFac`) are named in `\lean{}` hints and the objectwise lemma is proved.

2. **Functor-category promotion:** `CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` lifts the objectwise colimit to a colimit in the functor category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`. This step is explicit in the proof text: "apply `evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` to promote the objectwise colimit data".

3. **Apex identification:** `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj` establishes the canonical identification `(M ⊗ N).obj U = M.obj U ⊗[S.obj U] N.obj U` that matches the functor-category colimit apex to the concrete tensor product presheaf. The proof uses this to complete the iso.

The proof is granular enough: each step has a named Mathlib anchor, the transition between objectwise data and the functor-category result is explicit, and the apex identification is spelled out. A prover can formalize this without further decomposition.

**Q: Do the `\mathlibok` anchors name real Mathlib decls?**

| Anchor label | Lean name | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `lem:mathlib_tensorProduct_liftAddHom` | `TensorProduct.liftAddHom` | ✓ REAL |
| `lem:mathlib_evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` | `CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` | ✓ REAL |
| `lem:mathlib_tensorObj_obj` | `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj` | ✓ REAL |

All three are verified real Mathlib declarations.

**Minor note (non-blocking):** The proof of `lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit` invokes "J.W is monoidal for ℤ-tensor". This is a non-trivial claim (the class of local-isomorphisms is monoidal for the tensor product of sheaves of modules over a fixed base ring). It is mathematically sound — it follows from the fact that ℤ-flatness is preserved by the localization functor and the unit of sheafification is an iso on representable sheaves — but the proof text does not spell it out. This is a **soft flag** (not a blocker for the SNAP prover, which is working on the tensor-coequalizer piece, not the sheafification-unit piece), but a blueprint writer pass should add 1–2 sentences justifying the monoidal claim before that lemma is formalized.
